players.name: Vinicius Junior
players.ai_rationale: Brazil's primary creator vs. Morocco's compact mid-block. Vini hits 1g+a in ~60% of starts against bottom-50% xGA defenses, but Morocco specifically suppresses 1v1 dribblers via early double-teams. Marginal pick.

players.name: Rodrygo
players.ai_rationale: Rodrygo's shot map this season has been mid-range heavy. Two SoT is well within range against Morocco's deeper line, but he tends to fade if Brazil dominates possession early.

players.name: Casemiro
players.ai_rationale: The workhorse target. Casemiro averages 3.2 tackles vs. tier-1 opposition with 86% completion. Reliable floor; rarely sub'd in tight matches.

players.name: Marquinhos

target.human: Keep a clean sheet or score

players.ai_rationale: Defender's OR-clause target. Morocco's chance creation has been low this cycle, and Marquinhos is a real set-piece threat — he scored 3 in his last 12 internationals.

players.name: Kylian Mbappé
players.ai_rationale: The brutal pick. Mbappé hits ≥2g in only ~22% of starts. Argentina's CB pairing is Romero+Otamendi — one of the few defenses that genuinely cuts off his diagonal runs. High ceiling, very high risk.

players.name: Antoine Griezmann
players.ai_rationale: Griezmann's playmaker role makes 1g+a achievable (he hits it in ~55% of starts), but if Deschamps drops him deeper to control midfield, his final-third involvement collapses.

players.name: Aurélien Tchouaméni
players.ai_rationale: Argentina's midfield trio (De Paul, Mac Allister, Enzo) presses aggressively. Tchouaméni's pass completion historically dips to ~82% in high-press games. Pass-accuracy clause is the risk.

players.name: William Saliba

target.human: Keep a clean sheet or score

players.ai_rationale: Argentina averages 1.8 goals per match this cycle. Clean sheet is unlikely. Saliba has 0 international goals — the OR-clause is a long shot.

players.name: Lionel Messi
players.ai_rationale: Even at 38, Messi hits 1g+a in ~72% of competitive starts. France's RB rotation is unsettled. Target is on the easier side for a star — but a single tactical adjustment from Deschamps could neutralize him.

players.name: Julián Álvarez
players.ai_rationale: Álvarez's brace-rate is ~18% vs tier-1 defenses. France's CB pairing is solid but Saliba sometimes drops too deep, opening channels. Realistic in a 3-3 type match, unlikely in a tight defensive game.

players.name: Alexis Mac Allister
players.ai_rationale: France press in waves rather than constantly. Mac Allister thrives in this rhythm — his pass completion vs. French clubs in CL play averages 89%. High confidence.

players.name: Cristian Romero

target.human: Keep a clean sheet or score

players.ai_rationale: France's xG per match this cycle is 1.6. Clean sheet is unlikely but Romero is one of the few CBs with a real set-piece goal threat (4 in his last 18 club appearances).

players.name: Achraf Hakimi
players.ai_rationale: Morocco's most reliable creator. Brazil's LB rotation is unsettled and Vinicius rarely tracks back — Hakimi will have acres of space. Strong pick.

players.name: Hakim Ziyech
players.ai_rationale: Ziyech's set-piece deliveries plus his trademark cut-inside-and-shoot pattern make 2 SoT realistic against any defense. Risk: he gets subbed off in the 65th if Morocco needs to defend a lead.

players.name: Youssef En-Nesyri
players.ai_rationale: Single-goal target is generous, but Morocco's chance creation against top-tier opposition is the lowest in the field. En-Nesyri averages 0.6 npxG vs. tier-1 defenses.

players.name: Yassine Bono
players.ai_rationale: Bono averages 4.2 saves per match against top-10 opposition. Brazil will generate volume — the saves clause is the easier path. Strong dark-horse pick.
